rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the target component or implementation scope, following AADL structural rules
Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use valid component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes when referring to component ports
Use implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings after relevant subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names are not AGREE variables
AGREE assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports should be placed in valid component type scopes
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
